state_transitions.trigger: start() called — initiates ScannerService gRPC server on configured listen address
gRPC server bound and accepting connections
startup_timeout_ms exceeded (10s) or port bind failure
No device heartbeats received within 3× health_check_interval
stop() called — graceful gRPC server shutdown, drain pending batches

params.name: gateway_listen_host
params.description: Host address for the ScannerService gRPC server (Android-facing). Binds to all interfaces by default.

params.name: gateway_listen_port
params.description: Port for the ScannerService gRPC server (Android-facing).

params.name: wms_adapter_id
params.description: Adapter ID of the WMS spoke for barrel scan routing. Must match a registered Forge adapter.

params.name: ims_adapter_id
params.description: Adapter ID of the IMS spoke for asset scan routing. When null, INSPECTION/INVENTORY route to WMS only and asset operations are unroutable.

params.name: qms_adapter_id
params.description: Adapter ID of the QMS spoke for sample scan routing. Future — when null, SAMPLE_COLLECT/SAMPLE_BIND are unroutable.

params.name: device_token_secret
params.description: Secret key for signing and verifying device authentication tokens. HMAC-SHA256 symmetric key.

params.name: max_batch_size
params.description: Maximum number of scan events per SubmitScanBatch RPC call. Batches exceeding this are rejected.

data_sources.name: submit_scan_batch
data_sources.description: Batch scan event submission from Android devices. Each batch contains 1-max_batch_size ScanEvent messages with barcode value, scan type, operator, device, and location. Gateway translates each event to a ContextualRecord.

data_sources.name: pull_delta
data_sources.description: Device-initiated delta pull. Android devices request changes since their last sync timestamp - barrel movements, status updates, job assignments. Scoped by DeltaScope (ASSIGNED_JOBS, LOCATION_BARRELS, ALL).

data_sources.name: heartbeat
data_sources.description: Device health heartbeat. Reports battery level, GPS coordinates, firmware version, scan count since last heartbeat. Gateway uses this for device fleet health monitoring.

data_sources.name: register_device
data_sources.description: Device registration and identity provisioning. Each Keyence BT-A500GA receives a device_id, auth token, and permitted scan types upon first registration.

data_sources.name: scan_event_stream
data_sources.description: Subscribe-mode view of SubmitScanBatch — scan events are pushed to hub subscribers as they arrive from devices. Same data as push mode but framed as a continuous subscription for tag-based filtering.

mappings.description: Unique scan event identifier assigned by the gateway upon receipt.

mappings.transform: normalize:SCAN_TYPE_ prefix stripped, mapped to dotted domain path (barrel.entry, asset.receive, sample.collect)
mappings.description: Normalized scan type from scanner.v1.ScanType enum (12 values across 3 domains).
Raw barcode/QR content captured by the scanner. May contain barrel ID, asset tag, or sample label depending on scan type.
Registered device identifier (e.g., DEV-KEYENCE-001). Maps to equipment_id in RecordContext.
Operator badge or login ID. Maps to operator_id in RecordContext.
Human-readable location string (Warehouse-A/Bay-3). Maps to area in RecordContext.
Associated WMS job ID when scan is part of a warehouse operation. Maps to batch_id in RecordContext.
Production batch ID for QMS sample scans.
IMS asset identifier for asset-type scans (ASSET_RECEIVE, ASSET_MOVE, ASSET_INSTALL).

routing_rules.description: WMS barrel operations — single-target routing to WMS spoke.
Dual-target routing — barrel tracking (WMS) and asset compliance (IMS).
IMS asset operations — single-target routing to IMS spoke.
QMS sample operations — single-target routing to QMS spoke (future).

scan_rejection.description: Individual scan events within a batch may be rejected (ScanRejection) without failing the entire batch. Rejected scans are returned to the device for retry or operator intervention.

dead_letter.description: Undeliverable scan events (unroutable scan type, all target spokes down) are sent to dead letter queue for manual review.

integrity.notes: Phase 1 scaffold. Two-tier architecture: Android devices → ScannerService gRPC server (gateway) → Forge Hub. Multi-spoke routing based on ScanType classification. First Forge edge device adapter. Backfill not supported — scan events are ephemeral, authoritative records live in target spokes.
